title: Reduce evaluation startup overhead from repeated CloudWatch/logging init
description:
## Goal
Reduce startup latency in CLI evaluations by removing redundant logging/cloudwatch initialization and avoid Watchtower empty-message warnings.

## Scope
- Identify repeated CloudWatch handler setup in evaluation startup path
- Add safeguards to avoid redundant initialization
- Eliminate empty log messages that trigger Watchtower warnings
- Validate with targeted checks/tests

## Definition of Done
- Startup path avoids unnecessary CloudWatch reinit
- Watchtower empty-message warning source removed
- Targeted validation recorded

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Started implementation. Investigating repeated setup paths in CustomLogging.set_log_group/setup_logging and Cloudwatch client initialization, plus empty log messages that trigger WatchtowerWarning.

comments.author: derek
comments.text:
Implemented startup/logging optimizations:
1) CloudWatchLogger now reuses a shared boto3 CloudWatch client across instances (cache keyed by runtime credential mode + region), preventing repeated client initialization during evaluation startup.
2) CustomLogging.set_log_group now no-ops when requested log group is already active, avoiding unnecessary handler rebuilds.
3) Removed empty logging.info("") calls in CLI evaluation path, which were producing WatchtowerWarning: Received empty message.

Validation:
- python -m py_compile plexus/CustomLogging.py plexus/plexus_logging/Cloudwatch.py plexus/cli/evaluation/evaluations.py
- /home/derek/miniconda3/envs/py311-dev/bin/pytest -q plexus/plexus_logging/Cloudwatch_test.py (4 passed)
- /home/derek/miniconda3/envs/py311-dev/bin/pytest -q plexus/tests/test_evaluation_commands.py (12 passed)

comments.author: derek
comments.text: Landed on branch fix/evaluations-speed in commit ffed369f. Includes shared CloudWatch client reuse, set_log_group no-op when unchanged, and removal of empty evaluation log messages that triggered Watchtower empty-message warnings.
